skill.name: write-a-skill
skill.description: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
skill.summary: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation guide for creating agent skills. All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown inline code syntax for file paths, not shell execution; 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are template placeholders and benign references to content hashing. No malicious code, network requests, or command execution is present.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Template Placeholder Misidentified
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged '[specific triggers]' as weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a template placeholder in skill description examples, not cryptographic code. No security risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching error - 'hash' references are about skill metadata content hashing, and bracket syntax is template placeholders in documentation examples.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misidentified
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick characters in markdown as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are markdown inline code fences for file paths like `SKILL.md` and `helper.js`. No command execution occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks appear within markdown code blocks showing directory structures and file names. No shell or Ruby command execution is present in the skill content.

content.user_title: Create new agent skills with structure
content.value_statement: Creating agent skills from scratch is difficult without proper structure. This skill provides templates, workflows, and best practices so you can build reusable skills with clear descriptions, proper file organization, and progressive disclosure.
actual_capabilities: Generate complete SKILL.md files with proper frontmatter and structure
Create skill directory layouts with main files and subdirectories
Write skill descriptions that trigger correctly based on user prompts
Apply progressive disclosure patterns to manage skill complexity
Add utility scripts for deterministic operations when needed
Split content across multiple files for skills exceeding 100 lines
limitations: Cannot execute or test skills - only creates structure and documentation
Does not validate that generated skills work in target environments
Does not provide domain-specific knowledge for skill content
Script generation is limited to simple utility patterns

use_cases.title: Building a new skill from requirements
use_cases.description: Start with a user's vague idea and transform it into a fully structured skill with proper triggers, workflows, and examples.

use_cases.title: Organizing complex skill content
use_cases.description: Break down oversized skills into main instructions with progressive disclosure via linked REFERENCE and EXAMPLES files.
use_cases.target_user: Skill authors with extensive documentation needs

use_cases.title: Standardizing team skill development
use_cases.description: Establish consistent skill structure across a team using the provided templates and review checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the write-a-skill skill to create a skill for [domain/topic]. Focus on [specific use case].

prompt_templates.title: Create a skill with multiple files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the write-a-skill skill to create a skill that covers [main topic] with advanced features for [subtopic]. The main SKILL.md should stay under 100 lines.

prompt_templates.title: Create a skill with utility scripts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the write-a-skill skill to create a skill that includes utility scripts for [operation]. Include both the script and instructions for when to use generated code vs the script.

prompt_templates.title: Review and improve an existing skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the write-a-skill skill to review [existing skill content] and suggest improvements for structure, trigger clarity, and progressive disclosure.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for extracting data from API responses
output_examples.output: Generates SKILL.md with description, quick start example, workflow steps for parsing JSON/XML, and references to utility scripts for common transformations

output_examples.input: Create a skill that needs 300 lines of documentation
output_examples.output: Generates main SKILL.md (under 100 lines) with overview and quick start, plus separate REFERENCE.md for detailed workflows and EXAMPLES.md for use cases

output_examples.input: Create a skill for code formatting with utility scripts
output_examples.output: Generates skill with bundled scripts/format.js for deterministic formatting, instructions in SKILL.md explaining when to use script vs generated code

best_practices: Write descriptions with specific trigger phrases that distinguish your skill from similar ones
Keep main SKILL.md under 100 lines using progressive disclosure for advanced content
Use utility scripts for deterministic operations that would otherwise be generated repeatedly
anti_patterns: Using vague descriptions like 'helps with documents' that provide no trigger context
Putting all content in one file when it spans multiple domains or complexity levels
Creating scripts for non-deterministic operations that need human judgment

faq.question: What is the minimum required file for a skill?
faq.answer: A skill requires only SKILL.md with frontmatter containing name and description.

faq.question: How do I decide when to split content into multiple files?
faq.answer: Split when SKILL.md exceeds 100 lines, content has distinct domains, or advanced features are rarely needed.

faq.question: When should I add utility scripts instead of writing instructions?
faq.answer: Add scripts for deterministic operations like validation or formatting that save tokens and improve reliability.

faq.question: What makes a good skill description?
faq.answer: A good description is under 1024 characters, written in third person, with first sentence stating capability and second sentence listing specific triggers.

faq.question: Can this skill create executable code?
faq.answer: This skill generates structure and documentation. It can include script placeholders but does not write or execute code.

faq.question: How do I test a skill after creating it?
faq.answer: After creating a skill, manually test it by prompting the agent with the trigger phrases from your description.
